rulings.Case Title: RYAN PAWELL VS. EL RANCHO DEVELOPMENT COMPANY S2 ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2025, line 1. DEFENDANT EL RANCHO DEVELOPMENT COMPANY S2'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Both parties' Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. Plaintiff's declaration filed on November 12, 2024 is stricken, as not appropriate on a demurrer and Plaintiff's request to consider "evidence" submitted after the reply (November 20, 2024) is denied. The issue before the Court is NOT whether the building was subject to the retrofit requirements at any time. The issue is whether at the time the alleged misrepresentation was made the original landlord knew or should have known about the applicability of the San Francisco Mandatory Soft Story Retrofit Ordinance and failed to disclose or mispresented this fact. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to allege facts, not conclusions, to support the relevant allegations outlined above. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2025, line 3. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT DANICA HILL'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD, Defendant's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is DENIED. Defendant is in pro per in this case. Limited Scope counsel appeared in the matter for MSC and trial call. The date of MSC has passed, the trial was ordered off calendar and the action settled on November 4, 2024. The Court further notes that the proof of service of the order advancing the hearing date as to the defendant is defective. The proof of service appears to claim that Danica Hills is "attorney for Plaintiff" in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KEITH HENDLEY VS. THOMAS PLAGEMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Requests For Admissions That Were Deemed Admitted For Failure To Respond; Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2025, line 4. DEFENDANT THOMAS PLAGEMANN'S Motion To Withdraw Or Amend Requests For Admissions That Were Deemed Admitted For Failure To Respond. Defendant's Motion to Withdraw or Amend Request for Admission. Hearing Required to address defense counsel's review (or lack thereof) of the Court record at the time the representation of the defendant was taken on. The motion to deem admissions admitted was filed on December 13, 2024 at was viewable on the Court's register of actions at the time Defendant filed its substitution of attorney on December 17, 2024 at 9:24 a.m. Attached to the Motion to Deem Admissions admitted was a copy of the Request for Admissions and the proof of service. The motion was to be heard on December 20, 2024. The motion would not have been granted per the Discovery Act had the responses to Request for Admissions been provided at any time between 9:24 a.m. on December 17, 2024 and 9:30 a.m. on December 20, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 101 BRODERICK, LLC VS. CURTIS ROBERSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANT DAVID ROBERSON'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Demurrer is not a proper motion to raise factual issues not appearing on the face of the complaint. The Court will not consider evidence provided by either side as it is improper. Both parties are ordered to familiarize themselves with the proper procedures regarding any further motions filed in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
